---
title: "Pine Barren Frostweed (Crocanthemum corymbosum)"
slug: "crocanthemum-corymbosum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623532302/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pine Barren Frostweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malvales"
tax_family: "Cistaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malvales", "Cistaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623532302/medium.jpg" alt="Pine Barren Frostweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/aeheerema">
aeheerema</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342404340">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malvales
-   **Family:** Cistaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=856610)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and bright yellow, five-petaled flowers that open in sunny conditions. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub, sandhills, and open pine flatwoods. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is well adapted to fire-maintained ecosystems, often occurring as a subtle component of native groundcover.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/856610-Crocanthemum-corymbosum)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-crocanthemum-corymbosum/)
